stem: A team is building an online shop and uses decomposition.
model_answer: Decomposition is breaking a large problem into smaller sub-problems that can be solved (and tested) independently. For the online shop, the team can split the project into modules such as user accounts, product catalogue, shopping basket, checkout/payments, and order tracking. Each smaller problem is easier to design, code and test, and different developers can work on different parts at the same time.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain what decomposition is and one benefit of using it here.

mark_points.text: decomposition = breaking a large problem into smaller sub-problems/modules
accepted_alternatives: split a big problem into smaller parts that are easier to solve

mark_points.text: benefit: each sub-problem is easier to code/test OR different developers can work in parallel
accepted_alternatives: smaller parts are simpler to program and test
team members can work on different modules at the same time
